title: Route unprocessable events to a dead-letter queue
description: Configure DLQs/retry policies so poison events do not retry forever or vanish, and can be inspected and replayed.
body: Use when a function consumes async events (queue, stream, async invoke). Do not let a failing event retry infinitely (blocking the source) or silently disappear after exhausting retries. Actions: set a bounded retry/maximum-receive count, attach a dead-letter or on-failure destination, and build a redrive path to inspect and replay DLQ messages after a fix. Validation signals include a poison-event test landing in the DLQ and not blocking the stream. Risk boundary: a poison message on an ordered stream can stall all processing behind it; ensure failures move aside (DLQ) rather than blocking, and alert on DLQ depth.
